task: I’m comparing a couple of used car options and one of them looks good, but the seller wants a quick commitment. Can you help me make a simple go/no-go checklist so I don’t rush into something expensive?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a simple **go / no-go used car checklist**.

## GO only if all are true
- [ ] Seller full name matches title/registration
- [ ] VIN on title matches VIN on car
- [ ] Current registration is shown
- [ ] Title status is clear and explained
- [ ] Out-the-door price is in writing
- [ ] Deposit terms are in writing
- [ ] Live video walkaround is provided
- [ ] You’ve seen photos of title, VIN plate, and odometer
- [ ] Mileage makes sense
- [ ] Inspection is allowed or completed
- [ ] Payment method is safe and traceable
- [ ] You can meet at a bank, DMV, or public inspection lot

## NO-GO if any are true
- [ ] They won’t show the title
- [ ] They won’t show the VIN on the car
- [ ] They push a deposit before verification
- [ ] They dodge lien/title questions
- [ ] They only want weird payment methods
- [ ] They refuse a live video or inspection
- [ ] They won’t meet anywhere safe
- [ ] The story keeps changing

## Safe rule
**If title, VIN, and registration are not verified first, it’s a no.**

If you want, I can turn this into a **one-screen text version** you can keep open while messaging the seller.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, this is solid. I’d want one more layer though — the stuff that catches the rushed deal before it gets to the title part.

Add a quick **red flag / pause list**:
- price way below market with a hurry-up story
- seller says they’re out of town / can’t meet in person
- won’t send a cold start video or recent walkaround
- title is “at home,” “in the mail,” or in someone else’s name
- asks for cash app / Zelle / gift card type stuff
- pressure like “I’ve got another buyer today”
- no service history, but they claim everything is perfect

And maybe a simple **2-step rule**:
1. Verify title + VIN + registration
2. Get inspection + safe payment

If either step gets blocked, I’d call it no-go. I’ve seen how fast a “looks good” deal can turn sideways — after that Marketplace scam, I’m way less interested in moving fast just because someone says there’s another buyer.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s the tighter version.

# Used Car Go / No-Go Checklist

## Step 1: Verify title + VIN + registration
**GO only if:**
- [ ] Seller full name matches title/registration
- [ ] VIN on title matches VIN on car
- [ ] Current registration is shown
- [ ] Title status is clear and explained
- [ ] Title is in hand, not “at home” or “in the mail”

## Step 2: Inspection + safe payment
**GO only if:**
- [ ] Live video walkaround is provided
- [ ] Cold start video or recent walkaround is provided
- [ ] Inspection is allowed or completed
- [ ] Out-the-door price is in writing
- [ ] Deposit terms are in writing
- [ ] Payment method is safe and traceable
- [ ] You can meet at a bank, DMV, or public inspection lot

## Red flag / pause list
Pause or walk if:
- [ ] Price is way below market with a hurry-up story
- [ ] Seller says they’re out of town or can’t meet in person
- [ ] They won’t send a cold start video or recent walkaround
- [ ] Title is “at home,” “in the mail,” or in someone else’s name
- [ ] They ask for Cash App / Zelle / gift card / crypto / wire
- [ ] They pressure you with “another buyer today”
- [ ] No service history, but they claim everything is perfect
- [ ] The story keeps changing

## Simple rule
**If either step gets blocked, it’s no-go.**

## Short version
1. **Verify title + VIN + registration**
2. **Get inspection + safe payment**
3. **If blocked, walk**

If you want, I can turn this into a **one-screen text checklist** you can keep open while messaging the seller.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that’s the right shape. Tight enough to use while the seller’s pushing.

The only tweak I’d make is maybe add **"no weird excuses"** under the pause list — like if they keep dodging one step with a story, that’s basically the answer.

Otherwise, solid. This is usable.
